title: flaveria vaginata
intro: Flaveria vaginata, also known as tick trefoil, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It is a member of the daisy family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving pain, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antifungal activities. Flaveria vaginata can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves can also be dried and used in herbal supplements or tinctures.
